articles.title: Israeli court rejects appeal by detained Gaza aid flotilla activists
articles.description: An Israeli court on Wednesday rejected an appeal by two foreign nationals, one Spanish and one Brazilian, challenging their detention in Israel. The two men were seized in international waters last week while participating in a humanitarian aid flotilla seeking to break Israel’s blockade of the Gaza Strip. The ruling means an earlier decision extending their detention remains in place.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu Govt Formation: Vijay's TVK Reaching Magic Figure With Congress Support?
articles.description: The majority score in the Tamil Nadu Assembly, which has 234 members, is 118.

articles.title: White House may be planning to bring a law to ensure Anthropic, Google and other companies cannot say 'No' to the US government in future
articles.description: The US government is reportedly exploring new measures to compel AI firms like Google and Anthropic to cooperate on government use of their tools. Proposals include a vetting system for advanced AI models and restrictions on companies refusing government demands, potentially impacting national security applications and raising industry concerns about development speed.

articles.title: US nears consensus with Iran on one-page memorandum to end war
Will Vijay’s Numbers Fall In Line? The Majority Question
articles.description: Vijay, the chief of Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), has claimed to form the next Tamil Nadu government but lacks the necessary support of 118 MLAs, having won only 108 seats.

articles.title: One Vote, One History: Anand Mahindra Wants This TN Election Math Shown In Every Classroom
articles.description: Anand Mahindra praised the "power of one vote" after TVK's dramatic one-vote win in Tamil Nadu's Tiruppattur constituency.

articles.title: TVK At 112 with Congress Support, Still 6 Short As TN Governor Not Convinced Claim
articles.description: Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam chief Vijay’s bid to form the government in Tamil Nadu faces uncertainty as the party falls short of the majority mark. With 107 seats and support from Congress’ 5 MLAs, TVK’s tally stands at 112, still six short of 118. Congress has extended conditional support, insisting that “communal forces” be kept out. Vijay has written to Governor Rajendra Arlekar seeking time to prove majority. Meanwhile, the VCK is undecided, and Left parties and IUML have declined support, leaving the state in a tense political situation.

articles.title: Move to install statues of leaders from Nehru-Gandhi family questioned
articles.description: Opposition arises against installing Nehru-Gandhi family statues in Kalaburagi, citing lack of transparency and calls for broader representation.

articles.description: The development comes weeks after the BJP formed the government in Bihar, following Nitish Kumar’s move to the Rajya Sabha.

articles.title: A tale of two CMs: Mamata Banerjee refuses to step down, Himanta Biswa Sarma tenders resignation
articles.description: While the stage is set for the BJP to form governments in both Assam and West Bengal, the political scenes unfolding in the two neighbouring states couldn’t be more different.

articles.title: Can Mamata Banerjee Refuse to Resign? Rules Explained Amid Bengal Power Shift
articles.description: Constitutionally, a Chief Minister is not automatically removed after an election loss and can remain in office until resigning or being dismissed. While democratic convention calls for immediate resignation, the Governor can intervene. The Governor may ask the CM to prove their majority through a floor test in the legislative assembly.

articles.title: Supreme Court collegium approves names of 10 advocates as judges of Punjab and Haryana HC
articles.description: The Supreme Court collegium has approved the appointment of ten advocates as judges for the Punjab and Haryana High Court. The collegium also approved three judicial officers for the Andhra Pradesh High Court. These appointments mark a significant development in the judiciary. The names of the appointed individuals have been released.

articles.title: Congress Dumps DMK, Backs Vijay's TVK In Major Tamil Nadu Shift
articles.description: Congress has left its alliance with the DMK in Tamil Nadu and decided to support Vijay’s TVK, which emerged as the largest party but lacks a majority. With five MLAs, Congress will help TVK form the government, but only if it keeps “communal forces” out. The alliance, called “Mission 2029”, aims to continue long-term.

articles.title: Vietnam President To Lam accorded ceremonial welcome at Rashtrapati Bhavan

The visiting leader was warmly received by President Droupadi Murmu and Prime Minister Narendra Modi. Children dressed in traditional attire, holding the Vietnamese national flag alongside the Indian tricolour, also greeted him at the ceremony. President [...]

This development marks a decisive move away from politics, as EIMPA proclaims its non-political stance and will cease to support technician mandates from the Federation.

articles.title: Every 6th BJP MP in Lok Sabha won through 'vote chori': Rahul Gandhi
articles.description: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi on Wednesday alleged that every sixth BJP MP in the Lok Sabha secured his or her seat through "vote chori" and asked if they should be labelled "ghuspethiya" in that party's own parlance. Gandhi also claimed that the BJP would not be able to win even 140 seats today in the Lok Sabha if fair elections are held. "Through vote theft, sometimes individual seats are stolen, and at other times, an entire government. Of the 240 BJP MPs in the Lok Sabha, roughly every sixth MP secured seat through vote chori," the Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha said in a post in Hindi on X. "They are not hard to identify - should we, in the BJP's own parlance, label them 'ghuspethiya'? And what of Haryana? There, the entire government itself is an 'ghuspethiya'. The very institutions they keep in their pockets - the ones they manipulate to distort voter lists and the electoral process - are themselves 'remote-controlled'," Gandhi said, in a scathing attack on the ...

articles.title: 'Backstabbers...': DMK on Congress' possible tie-up with Vijay's TVK
articles.description: DMK spokesperson Saravanan Annadurai criticised their ally Congress, calling them a "backstabber" for supporting Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) after the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections. "We wish Vijay good luck when he takes oath on May 7th. Congress's decision to back the TVK feels like a betrayal. They claim they want to keep the BJP and RSS at bay, but their reasons are unconvincing. This move may create ripples across the country and raise questions among other alliance partners. If Congress is untrustworthy just one day after the election results, how will this affect leaders like Akhilesh Yadav and Tejashwi Yadav?" said Saravanan Annadurai.

articles.title: West Bengal on boil as BJP, TMC workers killed in post-poll violence
articles.description: The Election Commission of India (ECI) sought reports from local authorities and warned of strict action against those involved in violence.

articles.title: Breaking: Marco Rubio declares end of Iran operation, focus shifts to nuclear talks
articles.description: In a major geopolitical development, Marco Rubio has announced that the United States has formally concluded “Operation Epic Fury,” a military campaign launched against Iran on February 28, 2026. The statement signals a strategic shift as Washington now pivots toward diplomatic engagement, with a primary focus on Iran’s uranium enrichment program. Rubio confirmed that the operational phase has ended following notification to Congress, adding that future negotiations will center not only on enrichment levels but also on the fate of sensitive nuclear material stored by Iran. The move comes amid ongoing tensions in the Middle East, particularly around the Strait of Hormuz, where military activity has intensified in recent weeks. Meanwhile, Iran’s powerful military wing, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, has reportedly escalated hostilities, allegedly launching missiles and drones targeting areas including Fujairah in the United Arab Emirates. These developments have heightened regional instability and drawn sharp international reactions. Back in Iran, internal tensions appear to be rising. Reports suggest friction between President Masoud Pezeshkian and senior IRGC figures, including Ahmad Vahidi. Disagreements over military actions and foreign policy have triggered public statements from political aides, hinting at deeper divisions within Iran’s leadership. The United States, under Donald Trump, has also signaled plans to intensify its maritime blockade against Iran, with no defined timeline. As both nations exchange warnings, the world watches closely, with diplomacy and conflict hanging in a delicate balance.

articles.description: Barry Christian, a Republican state Senate candidate, was found dead in his truck at a wildlife management area after being reported missing. His ex-wife, Farrah, shared an emotional tribute on Facebook, reflecting on their relationship and his impact on their children and grandchildren. Barry was known for his friendly nature and dedication to improving Oklahoma.
